content.problemStatement:
Chef has an integer sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã. For each index iii (1‚â§i‚â§N1 \leq i \leq N1‚â§i‚â§N), Chef needs to divide AiA_iAi‚Äã into two positive integers xxx and yyy (ie. x,y>0x, y > 0x,y>0) such that x+y=Aix + y = A_ix+y=Ai‚Äã, then place this as a point (x,y)(x, y)(x,y) in the infinite 222-dimensional coordinate plane. Help Chef to find the maximum number of distinct points that can be put in the plane, if he optimally splits the values AiA_iAi‚Äã. Note that Chef can only perform one split for each index.

**Note:** Please use fast input/output methods for this problem.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the maximum number of distinct points there can be in the infinite plane.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: Chef can divide A1A_1A1‚Äã as (1,1)(1,1)(1,1), A2A_2A2‚Äã as (1,1)(1,1)(1,1), A3A_3A3‚Äã as (1,3)(1,3)(1,3), A4A_4A4‚Äã as (2,2)(2,2)(2,2), A5A_5A5‚Äã as (1,1)(1,1)(1,1), A6A_6A6‚Äã as (2,4)(2,4)(2,4). Ignoring duplicates, there are 444 distinct points, which is the maximum possible.
Test Case 2: Chef can divide A1A_1A1‚Äã as (8,8)(8,8)(8,8), A2A_2A2‚Äã as (4,4)(4,4)(4,4).
